zh.lifeSummary: 當Countess Elizabeth de Verdun於大約1306年出生在英國斯塔福爾阿頓時，她的父親Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun29歲，母親Maud Mortimer22歲。她於1320年6月11日與Bartholomew de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh在英國英格蘭素可結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1360年5月1日在英國薩默塞特享年55歲，埋葬在英國林可爾史爾林肯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Countess Elizabeth de Verdun于大约1306年出生在英国斯塔福爾阿頓时，她的父亲Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun29岁，母亲Maud Mortimer22岁。她于1320年6月11日与Bartholomew de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh在英国英格兰素可结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1360年5月1日在英国薩默塞特享年55岁，埋葬在英国林可爾史爾林肯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Verdun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places in France called Verdun. The placename is probably of Gaulish origin, deriving from ver(n) ‘alder’ + dūn ‘hill, fortress’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from Verdú in Catalonia (Spain), which has the same origin as the French name (see 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Verdon  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Virden
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Verdun  .

variants.name: Verdon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel, Pierre, Renaud, Sylvie.
French: habitational name from (Le) Verdon, the name of several places in various parts of France, for example in Dordogne, Gironde, and Marne. Compare   Ferdon  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Verdun, the name of a fief in Vessey (Manche), France. Compare   Verdun  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Countess Elizabeth de Verdun was born about 1306, in Alton, Staffordshire, England, her father, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, was 29 and her mother, Maud Mortimer, was 22. She married Bartholomew de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh on 11 June 1320, in Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 1 May 1360, in Somerset, England, at the age of 55, and was buried in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Countess Elizabeth de Verdun est née environ 1306, à Alton, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Maud Mortimer, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Bartholomew de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh le 11 juin 1320, à Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 mai 1360, à Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterrée à Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Countess Elizabeth de Verdun um 1306 in Alton, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun 29 und ihre Mutter Maud Mortimer 22. Sie hat am 11. Juni 1320 in Sussex, England, United Kingdom Bartholomew de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. Mai 1360 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Somerset, England und wurde in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Countess Elizabeth de Verdun è nata il circa 1306 nel Alton, Staffordshire, England, sua padre, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Maud Mortimer, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Bartholomew de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh il 11 giugno 1320, nel Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 1 maggio 1360, nel Somerset, England, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolta a Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Countess Elizabeth de Verdunが約1306年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアアルトンで生まれた時，父Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdunは29歳，母Maud Mortimerは22歳であった。1320年6月11日にイギリスイギリススセックスでBartholomew de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghershと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1360年5月1日にイギリスソメルセトで55歳で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアリンコルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Countess Elizabeth de Verdun(이)가 대략 1306년 영국 스태퍼드실르 알턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Mortimer(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1320년 6월 11일 영국 잉글랜드 수세스에서 Bartholomew de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1360년 5월 1일 영국 서머세트에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 링코실르 링코컨에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Countess Elizabeth de Verdun nasceu aproximadamente 1306, em Alton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Maud Mortimer, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Bartholomew de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh em 11 de junho de 1320, em Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de maio de 1360, em Somerset, Inglaterra, com 55 anos, e foi sepultada em Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Countess Elizabeth de Verdun родилась около 1306 г. в местности алтон, стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Mortimer, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Bartholomew de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh 11 июня 1320 г. в местности суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 1 мая 1360 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 55 лет и была похоронена в местности линколн, линколншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Countess Elizabeth de Verdun nació aproximadamente 1306, en Alton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Maud Mortimer, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Bartholomew de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh el 11 de junio de 1320, en Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 1 de mayo de 1360, en Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultada en Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.
